question_or_choice: How should Delta describe a domain scholar developing research software without prior formal Python proficiency?

ai_assistance.summary: AI agents helped distinguish a bounded development method from a generalized claim that programming expertise is unnecessary.

alternatives.option: Mention AI only in a generic disclosure.
alternatives.reason: This would hide the scholar-led method and the developer's actual starting position.

alternatives.option: Make vibe coding the main paper contribution.
alternatives.reason: It would displace the tool-first stylometry contribution.

alternatives.option: Define scholarly vibe coding as a secondary, evidence-gated development case.
alternatives.reason: It preserves human scientific ownership while documenting AI implementation support.

accepted_option: Define scholarly vibe coding as a secondary, scholar-led and evidence-gated development method.
rationale: The account is faithful to the development process without claiming that AI replaces programming expertise or that the model transfers to every scholar.
evidence_required: PromptEvent and Ticket coverage from P001 onward
HumanDecision records for method, claim, and acceptance choices
source_quote: Python öğrenmeden de var; sonuçta ben de tam olarak bilmiyorum, kendi önerdiğimiz scholarly vibe coding ile geliştiriyoruz.
provenance_notes: Transcribed from the current Codex thread. The interface did not expose a message-level UTC timestamp, so the ledger uses the P001 capture time and records the limitation.

question_or_choice: May Delta say that users can begin supported workflows without first learning R or Python?

ai_assistance.summary: Codex separated a structural no-prior-coding statement from unverified ease, learnability, or methodological competence claims.

alternatives.option: Ban all wording about learning R or Python.
alternatives.reason: It erases an intended product value and the developer's own experience.

alternatives.option: Claim the tool is easy for everyone.
alternatives.reason: No participant study supports general ease or learnability.

alternatives.option: Allow a bounded no-prior-coding statement while retaining method responsibility.
alternatives.reason: The interface can remove a coding prerequisite without proving general usability.

accepted_option: State that supported workflows require neither prior R/Python coding nor user-authored R/Python code, without claiming universal ease or method-free use.
rationale: This describes the technical prerequisite honestly while preserving the distinction between operating code and interpreting stylometric evidence.
source_quote: Python öğrenmeden de var; sonuçta ben de tam olarak bilmiyorum, kendi önerdiğimiz scholarly vibe coding ile geliştiriyoruz.
provenance_notes: Transcribed from the current Codex thread and linked to the bounded wording in ADR-0008.

question_or_choice: Should P002 also modify lemmata.app to add a Launch Stylometry entry?

ai_assistance.summary: Codex separated the parent-site product-family integration from the direct Delta workbench shell so that each change can have its own scope and evidence.

alternatives.option: Modify the live lemmata.app launcher during P002.
alternatives.reason: The parent site is a separate deployed surface and should receive its own integration, regression, and rollback evidence.

alternatives.option: Omit any relationship with Lemmata.
alternatives.reason: Delta belongs to the Lemmata tool family and should retain a recognizable product relationship.

alternatives.option: Design delta.lemmata.app now and add Launch Stylometry to lemmata.app in a later integration ticket.
alternatives.reason: This keeps P002 focused while preserving the future family-level launch path.

accepted_option: Build the direct Delta workbench in P002 and defer the lemmata.app Launch Stylometry integration to a later ticket.
rationale: The decision prevents silent changes to a separate live product and gives the later integration an explicit acceptance and rollback boundary.
evidence_required: A later parent-site integration ticket with link and regression tests
source_quote: nasıl ki lemmata.app sitesinde launch lemmata var ya altında da launch stilometry gibi bir şey düşünürüz ama o sonraki aşama.
provenance_notes: Native user decision captured in the current Codex thread; the parent-site change is deliberately outside P002.

question_or_choice: At what point should FAIR-oriented transparency enter Delta's development process?

ai_assistance.summary: Codex translated the user's requirement into ticket-level decision, prompt, test, run, failure, and evidence records rather than treating FAIR as only a final export feature.

alternatives.option: Document FAIR only when the public release is prepared.
alternatives.reason: Late documentation cannot reliably reconstruct decisions, failures, or scope changes.

alternatives.option: Publish source code without structured development provenance.
alternatives.reason: Source availability alone does not make decisions, claims, tests, or limitations transparent.

alternatives.option: Record FAIR-oriented provenance and evidence at every acceptance-gated stage.
alternatives.reason: This preserves findable, machine-readable, inspectable links among human decisions, AI assistance, code, tests, failures, and claims.

accepted_option: Make FAIR-oriented transparency continuous from each ticket opening through acceptance and handoff.
rationale: Continuous records reduce retrospective reconstruction and support both scientific auditability and the scholarly vibe coding case without claiming FAIR certification.
evidence_required: P002 Ticket, PromptEvent, HumanDecision, Run, test, and screenshot links
source_quote: FAIR ilkelerini unutma her şeyi yaptığımız aşamalar şeffaf olmalı.
provenance_notes: Native user requirement captured in the current Codex thread and applied as an acceptance discipline, not a release-only label.

question_or_choice: How should the completed P002 shell receive an independent second-model review before P003 begins?

ai_assistance.summary: Codex proposed a read-only multi-perspective Claude audit followed by evidence-backed repairs on an isolated branch, with Codex retained as the final reviewer.

alternatives.option: Let Claude freely redesign and edit main in one pass.
alternatives.reason: This weakens rollback, independent review, scope control, and before/after attribution.

alternatives.option: Ask Claude for a report but prohibit all repairs.
alternatives.reason: The user explicitly requested that verified shortcomings also be completed.

alternatives.option: Run an independent read-only audit first, then repair verified P002 findings on a dedicated branch and submit the branch to Codex review.
alternatives.reason: This separates diagnosis from implementation, reduces reviewer bias, preserves the accepted baseline, and supports adversarial verification.

accepted_option: Claude independently audits design, code, content, accessibility, security, and FAIR records, then fixes verified in-scope shortcomings on a separate branch for subsequent Codex review.
rationale: A second-model audit is most useful when it can challenge the current implementation without silently replacing the accepted baseline or expanding into P003 features.
evidence_required: Full P002 verification and clean-clone rerun
source_quote: şimdi claude code denetlesin eksikleri bulsun ve eksikleri tamamlasın
provenance_notes: Native user decision captured in the current Codex thread. The future Claude launch message must receive its own PromptEvent rather than treating this planning record as the execution request.

question_or_choice: Should Codex implement its merge-blocking and evidence-quality recommendations before the Claude P002 branch is accepted?

ai_assistance.summary: Codex separated code corrections, additive provenance repair, replayable evidence, and disclosed framework limitations into an isolated correction branch.

alternatives.option: Merge the Claude review branch unchanged.
alternatives.reason: Its Ticket graph, config-hash diagnosis, disabled-state evidence, and zoom wording were not sufficiently supported.

alternatives.option: Rewrite or delete the Claude evidence and Runs.
alternatives.reason: That would erase the historical audit state and weaken FAIR traceability.

alternatives.option: Preserve prior evidence, add scoped errata and superseding Runs, and implement the verified interface fixes on a Codex branch.
alternatives.reason: This resolves the defects while retaining the original evidence and a visible correction history.

accepted_option: Apply the Codex recommendations additively on a separate branch, then subject the result to a final adversarial review before merge.
rationale: The approach keeps scientific and development provenance inspectable, terminates self-reference at Git history, and does not convert unperformed zoom, screen-reader, or packet-capture tests into claims.
provenance_notes: Native user acceptance captured in the current Codex thread. decided_at_utc is the ledger capture time because the surface did not expose a message-arrival timestamp.

question_or_choice: Should the merge-ready P002 branch proceed through live product review, main integration, and then the bounded P003 secure-ingestion stage?

ai_assistance.summary: Codex stated the next sequence explicitly, retained main integration behind the live product gate, and kept P003 separate from the completed shell.

alternatives.option: Stop after the merge-ready branch and leave main unchanged.
alternatives.reason: The user asked to continue after receiving the branch, verification, and next-step summary.

alternatives.option: Merge immediately without a live product review.
alternatives.reason: The stated sequence required a final visual and interaction gate before main integration.

alternatives.option: Run the live product gate, record its limits, integrate P002 into main, and open P003 as a new bounded ticket.
alternatives.reason: This follows the explicitly stated sequence while preserving acceptance, rollback, and provenance boundaries.

accepted_option: Complete the live product gate, integrate the accepted P002 branch into main, and then open P003 secure ingestion separately.
rationale: The native continuation request follows an immediately preceding message that named these actions in order. The visual gate passed without a new P0, P1, or P2.
evidence_required: Separate P003 Ticket and PromptEvent link
provenance_notes: Native user continuation captured in the current Codex thread. The interpretation is context-bounded and does not authorize deployment, production data, or scope outside P003 secure ingestion.

question_or_choice: How should P003 use client-declared MIME when explicit upload role, extension, and role-specific content validation are already required?

ai_assistance.summary: Codex and an independent FAIR reviewer distinguished untrusted client MIME metadata from authoritative content parsing and identified an ASCII PDF-signature gap in the text parser.

alternatives.option: Reject every upload that omits a MIME declaration.
alternatives.reason: Client MIME is optional and untrusted; requiring its presence would not establish content type and would reject otherwise inspectable inputs.

alternatives.option: Ignore MIME even when the client supplies it.
alternatives.reason: A supplied contradiction between role and MIME is useful fail-closed evidence and should not be discarded.

alternatives.option: Require explicit role and extension, require supplied MIME to agree, and make the role-specific content parser authoritative even when MIME is absent.
alternatives.reason: This preserves deterministic fail-closed disagreement checks without mistaking client metadata for a security boundary.

accepted_option: Treat MIME as untrusted advisory metadata: when supplied it must agree with the explicit role; absence is allowed only because the role-specific content parser remains mandatory and authoritative.
rationale: The policy makes the acceptance criterion testable and honest. Known binary/document signatures are rejected by content regardless of MIME, while MIME absence does not become a type claim.
evidence_required: MIME disagreement and MIME-absent positive tests
PDF/RTF and BOM-prefixed document-signature negative fixtures
provenance_notes: The user's native acceptance of the audit recommendations authorized the bounded corrective implementation. This record captures the technical choice exposed by independent review; it is not final acceptance of the whole P003 ticket.
